title: Cyclosorus albociliatus
taxonomy: Cyclosorus albociliatus is classified within the family Thelypteridaceae, a group of ferns commonly known as the marsh ferns or thelypterid ferns. It belongs to the genus Cyclosorus, which is a genus of ferns native to tropical and subtropical regions. The species Cyclosorus albociliatus is further categorized as a species of flowering plant in the division Pteridophyta, the vascular plants. Its classification also includes the rank of a species within the kingdom Plantae and the phylum Pteridophyta. At the species level, it is a part of the family Thelypteridaceae and the genus Cyclosorus.
uses: Cyclosorus albociliatus, also known as the white-fringed fern, has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties. Its leaves have been used to treat fever, rheumatism, and other inflammatory conditions. In traditional medicine, the plant is believed to have antibacterial and antifungal properties, which are used to treat skin infections and wounds. The plant's extracts have also been studied for their potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. Further research is needed to confirm the efficacy of Cyclosorus albociliatus for medicinal purposes.
benefits: Cyclosorus albociliatus is a type of fern with potential health benefits due to its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. It may help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which can contribute to various chronic diseases. properties: Cyclosorus albociliatus, also known as the Silver Sedge, has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties.
